items.question: What does the kernel maintain across cells in a session?
options: in-memory state like variables and imports
items.answer: in-memory state like variables and imports
items.explanation: State persists until the kernel is restarted.

items.question: What does In [3] tell you about a cell?
options: it is the third cell on the page
it was the third cell executed
items.answer: it was the third cell executed
items.explanation: In [n] is the execution count, independent of page position.

items.question: What is the reliable way to verify a notebook is reproducible?
items.explanation: A clean kernel run top-to-bottom exposes hidden-state dependencies.

items.question: What does restarting the kernel do?
options: clears in-memory state but keeps your code
items.answer: clears in-memory state but keeps your code
items.explanation: Only variables/imports are wiped; cells and the saved file remain.
